Tried the larger base LM pivot on the original matched grouped-prefix winner: `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B` with `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, the 12-token grouped prefix, and the 53-frame cap. It still failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0, though CER improved to 0.773 relative to the plain 1.7B four-head run. So simply swapping in a larger text LM prior does not rescue the fragile grouped-prefix mainline within the 10-minute budget either.

Tested a larger-LM grouped transition pivot: `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B` with `batch_size=4` on the matched two-head grouped branch, using the strongest surviving transition design (`text_source=both`, 12-token grouped prefix, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, future weight 0.5, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). This recovered real life: asr_wer_prefix tied the robust best at 0.6667 and asr_cer tied the four-head robust baseline at 0.636. It is not a strict improvement and in-domain/gt secondaries were poor, but this is the first larger-base-LM grouped run to show a credible primary-metric signal rather than a clean regression.

Ablated mixed normalized+raw text from the new larger-LM exact-last grouped clue by rerunning `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B` / `batch_size=4` / 12-token exact-last grouped prefix with the default normalized text only instead of `text_source=both`. This failed badly: asr_wer_prefix fell back to 1.0 and transcript quality exploded (WER 5.33, CER 2.73), even though throughput improved to 12.3k samples. So mixed text appears important for the only larger-LM grouped setting that has shown life so far.

Exact reran the new larger-LM exact-last grouped clue (`llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). The earlier tie-level signal at asr_wer_prefix=0.6667 did not reproduce: this rerun collapsed to 1.0 with exploding WER/CER and only weak gt-ASR sanity. So the larger-LM exact-last grouped clue currently looks noisy rather than a robust new branch.

Tested whether the larger-LM four-head pivot benefits from the same fuller LoRA-target clue as the 600M baseline by combining `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B` and `batch_size=4` with `lora_targets=[q_proj,k_proj,v_proj,o_proj,gate,up,down]` on the usual four-head future-Q0, scheduled-sampling, EOS-weighting, 53-frame-cap recipe. It still failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with CER 0.818 and no in-domain recovery, so the noisy fuller LoRA-target clue does not transfer to the larger-LM plain baseline either.

Tried a distinct larger-LM structural pivot on the old fast non-AR Stage-1 path instead of the autoregressive branch: `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `use_lora=false`, `semantic_non_ar=true`, `text_source=both`, `semantic_loss_weight=2.0`, `acoustic_loss_weight=0.5`, and the p95-style 53-frame cap. It fit and still trained reasonably fast (~17.1k samples / 10 min), but the primary metric stayed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with CER 0.864 and no in-domain recovery. So simply giving the frozen non-AR upsampler a larger text LM prior does not revive that branch either.

Tested whether the fuller LoRA-target clue can rescue the stronger two-head exact-last grouped transition by adding `lora_targets=[q_proj,k_proj,v_proj,o_proj,gate,up,down]` on top of the 600M matched grouped branch (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This recovered some life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333, but it stayed well below both the robust 0.6667 baseline and the noisy larger-LM grouped tie, with only middling CER/in-domain metrics. So fuller LoRA does not obviously stabilize the two-head exact-last grouped branch either.

Tested whether the only live larger-LM grouped clue prefers a smoother late-boundary transition by combining `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, the matched two-head grouped branch, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, and the most promising late schedule `semantic_block_decode_schedule=3,3,3,1,2`. This failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with CER 0.864 and weak gt-ASR sanity, so the larger-LM grouped branch does not get stabilized by the same late-boundary schedule clue either.

Closed the last obvious larger-LM grouped clue stack by combining `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, the matched two-head 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and fuller LoRA targets `lora_targets=[q_proj,k_proj,v_proj,o_proj,gate,up,down]`. This failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.955 and no in-domain recovery, so fuller LoRA does not stabilize the noisy larger-LM exact-last grouped branch either.

Tested whether the two-head exact-last grouped branch is more step-limited than sample-limited by lowering `batch_size` from 8 to 4 on the plain 600M exact-last 12-token grouped recipe (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). This raised the update count substantially to 3260 steps in 10 minutes, but it still fell back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling secondaries. So simply buying more optimizer steps does not stabilize the exact-last grouped branch either.

Enabled a new exact-last decode regime in code where exact-last grouped steps can emit one more token than the number of future heads, then tested the first truly train/infer-matched two-head exact-last branch: `text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, `semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, grouped only for the first 12 semantic tokens, with scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This gives exact / future / future / exact grouped steps so both future heads are actually used during inference. It still failed on the primary metric at asr_wer_prefix=1.0, though in-domain WER recovered to 0.667. So simply unlocking a fuller two-head exact-last grouped step is not an immediate win, but the new code path works and is now testable.

Retested the new fuller exact-last decode regime on the only larger-LM grouped branch that had shown life by combining `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads, and a newly unlocked 4-token exact-last grouped step for the first 12 semantic tokens (`semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`). This makes the larger LM use both future heads during inference via exact / future / future / exact steps, but it still failed on the primary metric at asr_wer_prefix=1.0. CER was only 0.773, so the branch is not totally dead, yet the new fuller exact-last transition did not stabilize the noisy larger-LM clue either.

Implemented a new targeted train/infer-match path that only self-conditions the first grouped-prefix window after AUDIO_START: when scheduled sampling fires, replace just the first 12 Q0 inputs with a short free-running grouped decode from the pure text prompt (`semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`) on the original matched grouped recipe (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, plain 3-token grouped prefix, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). This was intended to be much cheaper than suffix-based free-running self-conditioning, but it was still catastrophically slow in practice: only 113 steps / 904 samples in 10 minutes, with asr_wer_prefix stuck at 1.0. So even a targeted early-prefix free-running match is too expensive in its current per-example form.

Retuned the newly unlocked fuller exact-last grouped family to a shorter early window: `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads, `semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, but only the first 8 semantic tokens grouped before falling back to exact decoding. This restored healthy throughput (~22.2k samples / 10 min), but the primary metric still stayed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with middling secondaries. So the new fuller exact-last family does not appear to come alive just by shortening the grouped window from 12 to 8 tokens.

Extended the newly unlocked fuller exact-last family to a true three-head match: `text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=3`, `semantic_block_decode_size=5`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, grouped only for the first 10 semantic tokens, with scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This creates exact / future / future / future / exact grouped steps so all three future heads are used during inference, but it still returned asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling CER/in-domain metrics. So the broader exact-last family beyond the old 3-token branch still looks stale.

Added a heavy-eval-only candidate reranker that compares the current blockwise semantic decode against the same decode with `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token` toggled, scores both under teacher-forced stage-1 semantic NLL, and chooses the lower-loss candidate. The first test on the original `text_source=both` / two-head / 12-token grouped-prefix recipe crashed with a JAX ConcretizationTypeError during heavy eval because the reranker tried to convert traced candidate lengths to Python ints inside the generation path. This needs a JAX-safe selection path before the heuristic can be judged.

Fixed the new heavy-eval candidate reranker to stay JAX-safe, then tested it on the original grouped-prefix winner: `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token grouped prefix, 53-frame cap, plus `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true` to compare the plain grouped decode against the same decode with `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token` toggled and pick the lower semantic NLL candidate. It finished cleanly but still returned asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with no in-domain recovery, so simple self-NLL reranking between the two main grouped transition styles does not rescue the noisy grouped branch.

Tested the new self-NLL heavy-eval reranker on the 600M exact-last grouped clue: `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap, plus `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true` to compare exact-last vs plain grouped decoding and choose the lower semantic NLL candidate. This tied the robust best primary metric at asr_wer_prefix=0.6667 while improving CER sharply to 0.545, which is the strongest character-level result seen in this family so far. It is not a strict primary improvement, but it is the first sign that simple heavy-eval reranking between grouped transition styles may actually stabilize a live branch rather than just regress.

Exact reran the promising self-NLL heavy-eval reranker on the 600M exact-last grouped branch (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap, `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true`). The earlier tie-level result at asr_wer_prefix=0.6667 with CER 0.545 did not reproduce: this rerun fell back to 1.0, though in-domain prefix WER improved sharply to 0.222. So the reranker currently looks like another noisy grouped-side clue rather than a robust stabilizer.

Tested whether the new self-NLL heavy-eval reranker can stabilize the only live larger-LM grouped clue by combining `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads, the 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true` to compare exact-last vs plain grouped decoding at eval. This still failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only modest CER 0.773, so the reranker does not obviously rescue the noisy larger-LM grouped branch either.

Extended the heavy-eval self-NLL reranker to compare a third grouped candidate with the late-boundary schedule `3,3,3,1,2`, then tested it on the 600M exact-last grouped clue (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap, `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true`, `semantic_eval_compare_schedule_candidate=3,3,3,1,2`). This three-way reranking between plain grouped, exact-last grouped, and the main late-boundary schedule still returned asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with no useful secondary recovery. So broadening the reranker candidate set to include the late-boundary schedule does not stabilize the grouped branch either.

Retested the broadened heavy-eval self-NLL reranker with the other late-boundary schedule clue by comparing plain grouped, exact-last grouped, and the schedule `3,3,2,1,2` on the 600M exact-last grouped branch (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap, `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true`, `semantic_eval_compare_schedule_candidate=3,3,2,1,2`). This failed even harder than the `3,3,3,1,2` variant: asr_wer_prefix stayed at 1.0 and transcript WER/CER exploded. So schedule-augmented self-NLL reranking now looks clearly stale as a family.

Reweighted the grouped exact-last branch toward Stage 1 by setting `semantic_loss_weight=2.0` and `acoustic_loss_weight=0.5` on top of the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped recipe (`text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This did not help the fast-convergence regime: asr_wer_prefix stayed at 1.0 with only middling secondary metrics, so simply emphasizing stage-1 loss is not a win for the exact-last grouped branch either.

Reweighted the original grouped-prefix winner toward Stage 1 by setting `semantic_loss_weight=2.0` and `acoustic_loss_weight=0.5` on top of the plain 600M matched grouped recipe (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, future weight 0.5, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This still missed the primary metric at asr_wer_prefix=1.0, though CER recovered to 0.682 and in-domain WER to 0.556. So simple stage-1 loss upweighting does not stabilize the fragile original grouped branch either.

Tested whether protecting the fragile grouped semantic branch from stage-2 feedback helps by setting `detach_semantic_hidden_for_stage2=true` on the original matched grouped recipe (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, future weight 0.5, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This still failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling CER/in-domain metrics, so stage2-gradient detachment does not rescue the grouped-prefix branch either.

Tested a smaller Stage-2 residual decoder on the fragile original grouped-prefix winner by shrinking the residual transformer to 2 layers / 2 heads / MLP 256 while keeping `text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, future weight 0.5, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, the 12-token grouped prefix, and the 53-frame cap. This still failed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling CER/in-domain metrics, so simply shrinking Stage 2 does not rescue the grouped-prefix branch either.

Extended the heavy-eval self-NLL reranker to compare a third candidate that effectively does fully exact token-by-token decoding by setting `semantic_eval_compare_schedule_candidate=1` on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch (`text_source=both`, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap, plus `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true`). This let heavy eval choose among exact-last grouped, plain grouped, and a tokenwise exact candidate, but it still returned asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with weak CER/in-domain metrics. So self-NLL reranking against a tokenwise exact fallback also looks stale in this family.

Tested a text-prefix LM anchor on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch by adding `text_loss_weight=0.1` on top of `text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, the 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and the 53-frame cap. This recovered some life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 with decent CER 0.636, but it still stayed below both the robust 0.6667 baseline and the original grouped-prefix outlier, while throughput dropped sharply to only ~13.4k samples in 10 minutes.

Retuned the text-prefix LM anchor downward on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch by setting `text_loss_weight=0.05` instead of `0.1` while keeping `text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, scheduled sampling 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, the 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and the 53-frame cap. The weak life from `0.1` did not survive: this dropped back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.909 and no in-domain recovery, so a smaller simple text anchor does not stabilize the exact-last grouped branch either.

Tested whether the weak text-prefix LM anchor stacks with the only live heavy-eval reranker by adding `text_loss_weight=0.1` on top of the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch and `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true` (so heavy eval chooses between exact-last and plain grouped decoding). This combination failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with uniformly weak secondary metrics, so the simple text anchor does not make the grouped-style self-NLL reranker any more robust.

Tested whether the noisy fuller LoRA-target clue stacks with the only live grouped-style heavy-eval reranker by adding `lora_targets=[q_proj,k_proj,v_proj,o_proj,gate,up,down]` on top of the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch and `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true` (so heavy eval chooses between exact-last and plain grouped decoding). This failed badly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with exploding WER/CER, so fuller LoRA does not make the grouped-style reranker any more robust on the exact-last branch either.

Extended the targeted early-grouped-prefix self-conditioning path so it can fall back to the usual cheap one-step scheduled-sampling replacement on most examples, then tested a sparse mixed variant on the original matched grouped recipe (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, plain 12-token grouped prefix, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.1`, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). This still failed badly: asr_wer_prefix stayed at 1.0 and throughput remained catastrophic at only 106 steps / 848 samples in 10 minutes. So even the new sparse targeted early-prefix mix does not make per-example free-running grouped self-conditioning viable in this training path.

Added a cheap targeted early-prefix self-conditioning fallback: when `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true` but the expensive inference-matched path is disabled, replace only the first grouped-prefix window after `AUDIO_START` with teacher-forced first-pass Q0 predictions instead of a random suffix. Tested this on the original matched grouped recipe with `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, plain 12-token grouped decoding, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. Throughput recovered to a normal ~18.5k samples / 10 min, but the primary metric still stayed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with weak secondaries, so a cheap targeted early-prefix replacement alone does not rescue the grouped-prefix branch either.

Tested the new cheap targeted early-prefix self-conditioning fallback on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch: when `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true` and `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, replace only the first grouped-prefix window after `AUDIO_START` with teacher-forced first-pass Q0 predictions instead of a random suffix. On top of `text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, the 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and the 53-frame cap, this recovered some life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 with normal throughput (~16.3k samples / 10 min), but it still stayed below the robust 0.6667 baseline and had weak secondary metrics.

Retuned the new cheap targeted early-prefix self-conditioning objective upward on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch by applying it on every self-conditioned example (`semantic_self_condition_prob=1.0`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`) while keeping `text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, the 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and the 53-frame cap. The weak `0.8333` life from the 0.5-probability version did not survive: this fell back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling in-domain recovery, so fully saturating the cheap targeted early-prefix replacement overcooks the exact-last branch.

Tested whether the weak text-prefix LM anchor stacks with the new cheap targeted early-prefix exact-last training objective by combining `text_loss_weight=0.1` with `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, and `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0` on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch (`text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only modest CER 0.818 while throughput fell to ~12.3k samples / 10 min, so the text anchor does not strengthen the new cheap targeted early-prefix exact-last objective either.

Tested whether the noisy larger-LM exact-last grouped clue benefits from the new cheap targeted early-prefix self-conditioning objective by combining `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, the matched two-head 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and the cheap targeted early-prefix replacement (`semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`). This failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling CER 0.864, so the cheap targeted early-prefix objective does not stabilize the noisy larger-LM grouped branch either.

Tested whether the weak cheap targeted early-prefix exact-last training clue stacks with the only live grouped-style heavy-eval reranker by combining `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, and `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true` on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch (`text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with CER 1.0, so the grouped-style self-NLL reranker does not strengthen the new cheap targeted early-prefix exact-last objective either.

Added a more exact-last-matched cheap targeted early-prefix self-conditioning mode by replacing only the speculative grouped future-token slots inside the first grouped-prefix window, then tested it on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch (`text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This recovered weak life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 with improved CER 0.773 and normal throughput (~18.8k samples / 10 min), but it still stayed below the robust 0.6667 baseline.

Tested whether the new cheap targeted early-prefix self-conditioning can better match the original plain grouped branch by restricting replacement to grouped future-token slots only during the first grouped-prefix window (`semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`) on top of the original matched grouped recipe (`text_source=both`, 2 future-Q0 heads, future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, plain 12-token grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This still returned asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling secondaries, so the future-slots-only cheap early-prefix objective does not rescue the plain grouped-prefix mainline either.

Retuned the more exact-last-matched cheap targeted early-prefix objective upward on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch by applying the future-slots-only replacement on every self-conditioned example (`semantic_self_condition_prob=1.0`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`) while keeping `text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, the 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and the 53-frame cap. The weak `0.8333` life from the 0.5-probability version did not survive: this fell back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only modest CER 0.818, so saturating the future-slots-only cheap targeted replacement overcooks the exact-last branch too.

Tested whether the grouped-style heavy-eval reranker stacks with the more exact-last-matched cheap targeted early-prefix training objective by combining `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, and `semantic_eval_compare_exact_last_candidate=true` on the 600M matched two-head exact-last grouped branch (`text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, EOS weight 2.0, 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, 53-frame cap). This fell back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling secondaries, so the reranker does not strengthen the future-slots-only cheap targeted exact-last objective either.

Tested whether the more exact-last-matched cheap targeted early-prefix objective transfers to the only larger-LM grouped clue by combining `llm_repo=QWEN3_1_7B`, `batch_size=4`, `text_source=both`, the matched two-head 12-token exact-last grouped prefix, and future-slots-only cheap early-prefix replacement (`semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`). This still failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with only middling CER 0.864, so the future-slots-only cheap targeted objective does not stabilize the noisy larger-LM exact-last grouped branch either.

Tested a more truly train/infer-matched exact-last branch by combining the new future-slots-only cheap targeted early-prefix training objective with the unlocked two-head exact/future/future/exact decode path: `text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, `semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, grouped only for the first 12 semantic tokens, and the 53-frame cap. This tied the robust best primary metric at asr_wer_prefix=0.6667 with improved CER 0.727 versus prior block_size=4 failures, making it the first sign of life for the broader exact-last family once the training objective is matched to the speculative future-token slots. It is not a strict primary improvement, so discard for now, but this is a more interesting clue than the earlier 1.0 collapses on the broader exact-last path.

Exact reran the promising broader exact-last clue that paired future-slots-only cheap targeted early-prefix training with the unlocked two-head exact/future/future/exact decode path (`text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, `semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, 53-frame cap). The earlier tie-level `0.6667` did not reproduce exactly: this rerun landed at `0.8333`, though CER stayed strong at `0.636` and throughput was excellent (~28.1k samples / 10 min). So this broader exact-last + matched-future-slots objective looks more alive than before, but still noisy rather than robust.

Retuned the new broader exact-last + future-slots-only clue to a shorter early grouped window by keeping the matched cheap future-slots-only early-prefix training objective but reducing the unlocked exact/future/future/exact decode path from the first 12 grouped semantic tokens down to 8 (`text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, `semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). This fell back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.955, so the new life in the broader exact-last family does not simply come from shortening the grouped window.

Retuned the new broader exact-last + future-slots-only clue to a longer, more natural four-step grouped window by keeping the matched cheap future-slots-only early-prefix training objective but extending the unlocked exact/future/future/exact decode path from the first 12 grouped semantic tokens up to 16 (`text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, `semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). This regressed back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0, so the new life in the broader exact-last family still looks localized around the 12-token / three-step window rather than extending cleanly to the more natural 16-token / four-step schedule.

Tested whether the more exact-last-matched cheap targeted early-prefix objective extends to a longer speculative block by keeping the future-slots-only training match but using the unlocked two-head `semantic_block_decode_size=5` exact-last path (`text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, 53-frame cap). This failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.955, so the new matched-training clue does not simply extend to longer speculative blocks beyond the revived `block_decode_size=4` path.

Tested whether the future-slots-only cheap targeted early-prefix training objective can revive the true three-head broader exact-last family by combining `semantic_future_prediction_steps=3` with the matched `semantic_block_decode_size=5` exact/future/future/future/exact decoder, `semantic_block_decode_max_grouped_tokens=15`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, `text_source=both`, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This recovered weak life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 with decent CER 0.682, which is much better than the earlier clean 1.0 collapse of the plain three-head broader exact-last branch, but it still stayed below the robust 0.6667 baseline.

Retuned the revived three-head broader exact-last family to a shorter two-block window by keeping the future-slots-only cheap targeted early-prefix training objective but reducing the matched `semantic_block_decode_size=5` exact/future/future/future/exact decoder from the first 15 grouped semantic tokens down to 10 (`text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=3`, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). This still showed weak life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333, but CER degraded to 1.0, so the three-head matched branch does not obviously improve just by shortening the grouped window from 15 to 10 tokens.

Added a tighter train/infer-matched future-head supervision flag (`semantic_future_prediction_match_block_decode=true`) that only trains future-Q0 heads on grouped-step source positions and only for offsets actually consumed by the active fixed block decode policy, then tested it on the live broader exact-last clue: `text_source=both`, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, future weight 0.5, future-slots-only cheap targeted early-prefix matching (`semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`), `semantic_block_decode_size=4`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap. This cleanly regressed to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 despite decent CER 0.773, so restricting the future auxiliary to only grouped-step start positions does not stabilize the revived block_size=4 exact/future/future/exact branch either.

Retested the new grouped-step-matched future-head supervision flag on the older two-head exact-last 12-token grouped branch where the standard `semantic_block_decode_size=3` exact/future/exact decoder only consumes one speculative future slot per grouped step. The run kept `text_source=both`, future weight 0.5, `semantic_future_prediction_steps=2`, `semantic_future_prediction_match_block_decode=true`, future-slots-only cheap targeted early-prefix matching (`semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_self_condition_use_inference_policy=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_early_grouped_prefix=true`, `semantic_self_condition_match_grouped_future_slots_only=true`, `semantic_self_condition_inference_policy_prob=0.0`), `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This still failed cleanly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.955, so masking the future auxiliary down to only the exact grouped-step uses does not rescue the older exact/future/exact 3-token branch either.

Transferred the new Stage-2 Q0 train/infer-match path onto the reintroduced exact-last grouped transition by adding `stage2_match_stage1_input_q0=true` on top of `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, grouped decoding for the first 12 semantic tokens, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This failed badly at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with exploded CER 2.73, so matching Stage 2 to the Stage-1 input Q0 stream does not stabilize the exact-last grouped branch either.

Added a new cheap hierarchical Stage-1 auxiliary: keep the main semantic decoder autoregressive, but also train the lightweight non-AR text-to-frame Q0 branch as a parallel auxiliary (`semantic_non_ar_aux_loss_weight=0.25`) using the already-computed causal prefix hidden states, then tested it first on the reproducible four-head future-Q0 baseline with future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This tied the robust best at asr_wer_prefix=0.6667 on its first run, but with worse CER 0.864 and worse in-domain WER 1.11, so it is only a weak new structural clue rather than a keeper yet.

Transferred the new parallel non-AR Stage-1 auxiliary onto the original grouped-prefix winner by adding `semantic_non_ar_aux_loss_weight=0.25` while keeping `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, grouped decoding for the first 12 semantic tokens, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This recovered only weak life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 with CER 0.818 and middling in-domain WER 0.667, so the auxiliary text-to-frame branch is not an immediate grouped-prefix rescue at this first weight.

Retuned the new parallel non-AR Stage-1 auxiliary downward on the reproducible four-head future-Q0 baseline by lowering `semantic_non_ar_aux_loss_weight` from 0.25 to 0.1 while keeping future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This collapsed badly to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with exploded CER 2.73, so the first tie-level result at weight 0.25 already looks noisy rather than like a robust new auxiliary direction.

Exact reran the most promising new parallel non-AR Stage-1 auxiliary setup on the reproducible four-head future-Q0 baseline (`semantic_non_ar_aux_loss_weight=0.25` with future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap). The first run had tied the robust best at 0.6667, but this exact rerun collapsed all the way to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with exploded CER 2.73 despite very strong throughput (~29.7k samples / 10 min), so the auxiliary non-AR branch now looks like another noisy side clue rather than a robust new structural win.

Added a new grouped-generation length-aware EOS heuristic by ramping a positive EOS logit bias over the final quarter of the allowed semantic-length budget (`semantic_eos_length_bias=2.0`), then tested it on the original grouped-prefix winner with `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, grouped decoding for the first 12 semantic tokens, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This still failed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with no EOS emitted, so a simple length-aware EOS bias does not rescue the grouped-prefix branch in this first form.

Tried a new structural future-head redesign: replaced the plain linear future-Q0 heads with offset-conditioned adapter heads that reused the shared audio token output head, aiming to make multi-token prediction converge faster from the main Stage-1 logits. The first run on the reproducible four-head baseline crashed in the future-head auxiliary path with `ValueError: attempt to get argmax of an empty sequence`, caused by the shared-head Q0 logit slice resolving incorrectly under this first implementation. Needs a safer future-head parameterization before the idea can be judged.

Fixed the new future-head redesign into a safer low-rank nonlinear MLP head (embed -> bottleneck 128 -> CODEBOOK_SIZE) and tested it on the reproducible four-head future-Q0 baseline in place of the original plain linear future heads, while keeping future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This ran cleanly but failed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with CER 0.864, so the cheaper nonlinear future-head parameterization does not improve the robust baseline in its first form.

Transferred the new low-rank nonlinear future-Q0 head redesign (embed -> bottleneck 128 -> CODEBOOK_SIZE per future step) onto the original grouped-prefix winner while keeping `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, grouped decoding for the first 12 semantic tokens, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap. This recovered real life at asr_wer_prefix=0.6667 with better-than-usual grouped-branch CER 0.727, but it only ties the robust baseline rather than beating it, so it is a promising new grouped-prefix-side structural clue rather than a keeper yet.

Exact reran the new low-rank nonlinear future-Q0 head redesign on the original grouped-prefix winner (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, grouped decoding for the first 12 semantic tokens, EOS weight 2.0, and the 53-frame cap). The first run had shown tie-level life at 0.6667, but this exact rerun collapsed to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with exploded CER 4.32 and degraded gt-ASR sanity, so the cheap nonlinear future-head family already looks like another noisy grouped-side clue rather than a robust improvement.

Added a new text-prefix semantic-length-delta predictor auxiliary (`semantic_length_delta_predictor_loss_weight=0.1`) that learns how many frames to add or subtract from the current heuristic cap, then tried it first on the reproducible four-head future-Q0 baseline. The first implementation crashed in heavy eval with a JAX `ConcretizationTypeError` because inference tried to turn the predicted length delta into a Python int while traced. Needs a JAX-safe runtime cap before the idea can be judged.

Retried the new text-prefix semantic-length-delta predictor auxiliary after fixing the JAX tracing issue, testing it first on the reproducible four-head future-Q0 baseline with `semantic_length_delta_predictor_loss_weight=0.1` plus future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, and the p95-style 53-frame heuristic cap. The learned cap did change behavior (heavy eval shortened to ~40/38 frames), but the primary metric still stayed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.955, so the first learned length-adjustment path is not an immediate robust-baseline win.

Transferred the new text-prefix semantic-length-delta predictor auxiliary onto the original grouped-prefix winner with `semantic_length_delta_predictor_loss_weight=0.1` while keeping `text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, grouped decoding for the first 12 semantic tokens, EOS weight 2.0, and the baseline 53-frame heuristic. The learned cap again shortened generation (to ~41/38 frames) and recovered only weak life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 while badly damaging in-domain behavior (`asr_wer_in_domain=3.56`), so the first learned length-adjustment path is only another noisy grouped-prefix side clue rather than a strong new cap policy.

Added a new local grouped-step lookahead for the reintroduced exact-last transition: on each 3-token grouped step (`semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`), compare the speculative middle future-token against the exact next-token candidate using a short 2-token exact-model lookahead, then keep whichever local path scores better (`semantic_block_decode_compare_exact_middle_candidate=true`). Tested it on the original grouped-prefix winner (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). It ran cleanly but collapsed to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with CER 0.864 and both prompts hitting the full 53-frame cap, so this richer local overlap/beam-style transition is not the corrective grouped-prefix fix in its first form.

Added a new text-anchored future-head redesign: each Stage-1 future-Q0 head now also receives a learned text-prefix logit bias derived from the prompt hidden states, scaled by `semantic_future_prediction_text_context_weight=0.5`, so speculative grouped tokens can stay more tied to the requested text. Tested it first on the original grouped-prefix winner (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). It recovered only weak life at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333, though CER improved sharply to 0.545, so the first text-anchored future-head variant is only a noisy grouped-prefix clue rather than a new keeper.

Retuned the new text-anchored future-head redesign down to `semantic_future_prediction_text_context_weight=0.25` on the original grouped-prefix winner (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). The first run at 0.5 had shown only weak life with strong CER, but this lighter version collapsed back to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.909, so the direct text-prefix bias on speculative future heads already looks like another noisy grouped-side clue rather than a stable grouped-token anchor.

Added a new inference-only recent-token repetition penalty for custom blockwise semantic generation (`semantic_recent_repeat_penalty=2.0`, window 8) to discourage grouped-prefix branches from repeating the same Q0 codes until the 53-frame cap. Tested it on the original grouped-prefix winner (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap). It ran cleanly and even improved 10-minute throughput to ~24.3k samples, but the primary metric still stayed at asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with both prompts hitting the full frame cap, so a simple recent-token anti-loop penalty does not rescue the grouped-prefix branch in this first form.

Added a new future-head distillation objective (`semantic_future_prediction_distill_weight=0.25`) that tries to teach each future-Q0 head to match the exact next-token Q0 distribution at the corresponding future position under the current Stage-1 hidden states, then tested it on the original grouped-prefix winner. The first implementation crashed at train time because the teacher-logit slice into the audio extra-head was wrong for Qwen's padded vocab/tokenizer alignment, producing an empty `(…, 0)` Q0-logit slice. This exposed a likely tokenizer-vs-model-vocab alignment bug around the added audio tokens, which is now the more promising structural issue to investigate directly.

Pivoted to a likely tokenizer/model-vocab alignment bug: Qwen's config vocab is padded above the live tokenizer length, so newly added audio tokens were landing partly inside the frozen base-vocab range instead of entirely in the dedicated extra-token path. After padding the tokenizer up to the model vocab before adding audio tokens and invalidating the dataset hash, the first baseline run crashed only because the new `train`/`unpadded` caches had not been regenerated yet. Regenerated the normalized-text dataset cache next; the alignment fix itself still needs a clean benchmark run.

Fixed a likely tokenizer/model-vocab alignment bug: Qwen's config vocab is padded above the live tokenizer length, so newly added audio tokens were previously landing partly inside the frozen base-vocab range instead of entirely in the dedicated extra-token path. The fix pads the tokenizer up to the model vocab before adding text/audio/Q0 tokens and invalidates cached train/unpadded datasets with the new ids. On the reproducible four-head future-Q0 baseline (future steps 4, weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, 53-frame cap), this improved the primary metric from the current 1.0 baseline to asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 and, more interestingly, finally produced EOS on both prompts with much shorter generations (~41/39 frames). It is still below the historical 0.6667 keeper, but this looks like a meaningful structural fix worth keeping and exploring.

Transferred the tokenizer/model-vocab alignment fix onto the original grouped-prefix winner (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, grouped decoding for the first 12 semantic tokens, EOS weight 2.0, p95-style cap). The fix again induced EOS on both prompts and shortened generation sharply to ~36/41 frames while improving in-domain WER to 0.556, but the primary metric only tied at asr_wer_prefix=0.8333 and gt-ASR sanity weakened to 0.118, so this first grouped transfer is a promising alignment-side clue rather than a new keeper.

Followed up the tokenizer/model-vocab alignment fix on the four-head future-Q0 baseline by forcing later EOS eligibility with `semantic_gen_min_new_tokens=48`, aiming to keep the new EOS-emitting regime from terminating too early (~41/39 frames). This removed the apparent benefit entirely: EOS disappeared again on both prompts, generation went back to the full 53-frame cap, and the primary metric regressed to asr_wer_prefix=1.0. So the alignment-fix gain does not survive a blunt minimum-length floor at 48 frames.

Retuned the tokenizer-aligned four-head future-Q0 baseline by lowering `semantic_eos_weight` from 2.0 to 1.0, aiming to keep the new EOS-emitting regime while delaying premature stopping. EOS still fired on both prompts with short generations (~42/40 frames), but the primary metric regressed to asr_wer_prefix=1.0, so the alignment-fix gain does not survive simply weakening EOS pressure.

Transferred the tokenizer/model-vocab alignment fix onto the reintroduced exact-last grouped transition (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, `semantic_block_decode_exact_last_token=true`, EOS weight 2.0, p95-style cap). The aligned branch still emitted EOS on both prompts and shortened generation to ~39/37 frames, but the primary metric regressed to asr_wer_prefix=1.0, so exact-last does not immediately combine with the alignment fix.

Revisited the future-head distillation idea after fixing the tokenizer/model-vocab alignment bug: each grouped future-Q0 head was softly distilled toward the exact next-token Q0 distribution at the corresponding future position (`semantic_future_prediction_distill_weight=0.25`) on the aligned original grouped-prefix winner (`text_source=both`, two future-Q0 heads at weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, first 12 semantic tokens grouped, EOS weight 2.0, p95-style cap). It now ran cleanly and still emitted EOS on both prompts, but the primary metric regressed to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 with worse CER 0.909, so this first soft exact-head distillation objective does not rescue the aligned grouped-prefix branch.

Transferred the earlier fuller LoRA target-set clue onto the tokenizer-aligned four-head future-Q0 baseline by adding the MLP down projection to the default targets (`lora_targets=[q_proj,k_proj,v_proj,o_proj,gate,up,down]`) while keeping future steps 4, future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, and the p95-style cap. The aligned branch still emitted EOS on both prompts and again reached only tie-level asr_wer_prefix=0.8333, with in-domain WER 0.556 but weaker CER 0.864 and even shorter generations (~31/35 frames). So the fuller LoRA target set remains only a noisy side clue on top of the alignment fix rather than a new keeper.

Retried the old mixed-text clue on top of the tokenizer-aligned four-head future-Q0 baseline (`text_source=both`, future steps 4, future weight 0.5, `semantic_self_condition_prob=0.5`, EOS weight 2.0, p95-style cap). Unlike the aligned normalized-text branch, this lost the new EOS behavior on the OOD prompt, went back to the full 53-frame cap there, and regressed cleanly to asr_wer_prefix=1.0 even though in-domain WER stayed decent at 0.556. So mixed normalized+raw text still does not transfer cleanly to the robust four-head branch, even after fixing tokenizer/model-vocab alignment.
